candidates.why: This is the obvious COGS-related D&A line, but it appears to be a component already embedded in the selected parent row 'Cost of Goods Sold'. The values reconcile with the nearby COGS breakdown, so adding it separately would double count.

variables.notes: Selected COGS parent bucket is 'Cost of Goods Sold'. The explicit COGS D&A row appears to be a sub-component of that selected row rather than a same-level separate expense to add later.

candidates.why: Although this is an SG&A-related D&A line, it appears embedded in the selected SG&A component rows. 'Selling and Marketing Expenses' reconciles to its balancing value plus this D&A row, so separate addition would double count.
This is administration-related D&A, but it appears to be a component already included in the selected parent row 'Administration Expenses', as shown by the balancing-value breakdown.

This is an explicit R&D D&A row, but 'Research and Development Expenses' is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, and the row appears to be embedded within that R&D expense via the balancing-value breakdown. Adding it separately would risk distortion/double counting.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists of 'Selling and Marketing Expenses', 'Research and Development Expenses', 'Administration Expenses', and 'Other Operating Expenses'. The explicit D&A rows for SGA, Administration, and R&D all appear to be nested components of those selected parent rows because each parent reconciles to a balancing value plus the D&A line. No separate same-level SG&A D&A row should be added.
